prompt.task: Validate a PRD chunk by analyzing its completeness, technical feasibility, and clarity for implementation by a development team.

context_loading.instructions: Before proceeding, read the context documentation inventory: - Read: .claude/docs/.context-docs-inventory.xml Based on validation focus, read relevant context: - For technical standards: Read architecture/system-design.md - For security requirements: Read security/authentication-patterns.md, security/authorization-patterns.md - For data validation: Read data/database-schema.md - For UI standards: Read ui/tailwind-standards.md

available_tools.description: You have access to the following tools for validating PRD chunks. Tool mappings are defined in .claude/tools/tool-mappings.xml and full documentation is in .claude/tools/available-tools.xml

tool.name: Read
tool.description: Read chunk content and validation criteria

tool.name: Bash
tool.description: Execute gh CLI for GitHub operations

tool.name: Grep
tool.description: Search for similar patterns in codebase

tool.name: github_mcp
tool.usage: Update chunk status, add validation feedback, check codebase

tool.name: context7_mcp
tool.usage: Verify technical feasibility of APIs and libraries

tool.name: code_reasoning_mcp
tool.usage: Analyze chunk completeness and identify gaps

available_tools.validation_guidelines: - Use Read to examine chunk content thoroughly - Use code_reasoning_mcp for systematic completeness analysis - Use context7_mcp to verify all technical references - Use Grep to check for similar implementations - Use github_mcp to update validation status - Use postgres_mcp/supabase_mcp for database requirement validation

step.title: Analyze Completeness
step.description: Review the chunk for missing requirements, unclear specifications, and incomplete technical details.

step.title: Verify Technical Feasibility
step.description: Use Context7 MCP to verify that specified libraries, APIs, and technical approaches are current and feasible.

step.title: Identify Dependencies
step.description: List cross-cutting concerns and dependencies that may affect implementation.

step.title: Assess Clarity
step.description: Evaluate whether the requirements are clear enough for developers to implement without ambiguity.

step.title: Provide Recommendations
step.description: Suggest improvements, clarifications, or additional requirements needed.

step.title: Store Results
step.description: Format validation results and store using GitHub MCP with appropriate status indicators.

step.title: GitHub Projects Update
step.description: - Update chunk issue (created with Feature Chunk template) with validation results - Add validation notes as issue comment with structured format - Update labels based on validation status: - If validated: remove "needs-validation" label, add "validated" label - If needs_revision: keep "needs-validation" label, add "needs-revision" label - If rejected: add "rejected" label - Update AAFD Stage custom field: Validation → Validated/Needs Revision - Update Priority if validation reveals scope changes - Store validation in: .claude/build/4-output/{epic-name}/3-validation/ - Move validated chunks to "Validated" column in Planning Board
